title: sorbus scopulina var. cascadensis
intro: Sorbus scopulina var. cascadensis, also known as Cascade Mountain Mountain Ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to western North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Sorbus scopulina var. cascadensis have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, offering relief from pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of ways.
